name: Compelled Duel
description: You attempt to compel a creature into a duel. One creature that you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is drawn to you, compelled by your divine demand. For the duration, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you, and must make a Wisdom saving throw each time it attempts to move to a space that is more than 30 feet away from you; if it fails this saving throw, it can't move away from you during that turn.
effect: Compels one creature (WIS save) to focus combat on you: disadvantage on attacks vs. others, and movement away from you is limited.
notes: Range: D&D 5e 30 ft; 1 grid (10 ft approximation is conservative — 30 ft = 2 grid; set 1 grid for melee paladin theme).
Movement-restriction and attack-roll debuff require condition/linked-targeting engine mechanic — see proposals.
Not substance-themed; paladin force-of-will enchantment — correctly free.
